body.title: Haiku completion audit: verify closed tickets were actually built (catch fake completions)
body.result: completion_audit.py built (list/log-result/summary commands, queries clan.memories, extracts criteria); 8 tests pass; day-close-audit Step 18.7 added; levers doc updated
body.intention: I intend that a closed ticket whose code was never written (a fake completion) is caught automatically, not discovered by accident.
body.description:
On 2026-06-05 the Granny dispatch handshake was found to be a fake completion: closed, docstring updated, code never written (T-granny-bus-flip-fake-completion). Fake completions are high-severity (you build on sand), invisible by definition (you don't know what else is fake), and cheap to check. Nothing currently verifies a closed ticket's completion criteria against actual code/state after close.

Add a recurring completion audit: for each recently-closed ticket, a Haiku subagent reads the ticket's **Completion criteria** and checks them against the actual repo/DB state, returning pass / fail / cannot-verify with a one-line reason. Failures surface as findings (draft tickets to re-open). Plug into the existing auditor device / day-close-audit rather than building a standalone one-off — it should run on a cadence (e.g. day-close) and be invokable ad hoc over a date range. This is the verification half of the ticket-quality loop: it also makes completion-criteria quality *matter* (the audit is only as good as the criteria), forcing better tickets without a separate mandate.

**Affected files:** the auditor device (devices/auditor/ or wherever day-close-audit checks are registered), a new completion-audit check module, possibly lab/claudecode/ helper to fetch closed-ticket criteria
**Design rules:** external-state-principle (audit reads durable state, not in-memory); no-sqlite
**Scope boundary:** In scope: per-closed-ticket criteria-vs-reality check via Haiku, findings output, cadence + ad-hoc invocation. Out of scope: auto-reopening tickets (surface findings, human decides); rewriting completion criteria retroactively.
**Completion criteria:** Running the audit over the last N closed tickets produces a pass/fail/cannot-verify verdict per ticket with a reason; deliberately seeding a fake completion (close a ticket whose criteria don't hold) is flagged as fail; integrated into day-close-audit so it runs on cadence.
